reviews.review_description: I'm not sure what others are experiencing, but I have the meta quest 2 and I've been practicing the Tai Chi two times a day for over a month and it has worked flawlessly for me. The only reason I am subtracting a star is because no matter what meditation scenario I choose, the moves are pretty much the same. The scenery is very well done, with a lot of different places to choose to exercise. I also fine the hand tracking to work well. I hope they come out with a new version with new moves to learn. I would like a harder challenge. As a side note, I have experienced better balance and less pain in my shoulders when I sleep. I look forward to doing my daily Tai Chi.

I just installed this app and did have a hard time calibrating hands in the tutorial. When I use the actual app for the lessons the tracking worked well.  But trying to set up it kept saying oh no, try again oh no, try again.

It has beautiful sceneries, I am going to make it part of my daily routine.

I find it difficult to stay inside the circle with my hands. I am so distracted by trying to do this that I'm completely missing the change in footing. Also, with the "push" forward, I have to reach beyond the boundaries to maintain the vibration.  For me this isn't Tai Chi, it's a game to try to follow the dots.  I expected verbal descriptions of each movement or their meaning, as with normal beginning tai chi classes. Nothing.

This app gives you the option of using the controllers or hand tracking. Using the controllers takes away the relaxed Tai Chi feeling because you have to grip the controllers. Well hand tracking was an absolute disaster, it works fine outside the app but it's useless in the app, in the end I had to give up and return to using the controllers. Also during a session I found the orbs often went to far away even though I'd selected small play area in settings. As hand tracking doesn't work I don't recommend anyone downloads this app.

reviews.review_title: Amazing app but... I Need Dev's help.
reviews.review_description: New hand tracking is not lining up properly. Please reach out to myself for assistance with this issue or contact Impact Labs for playtesters like myself to help troubleshoot and fine tune this amazing app. Would be an honor to help work on this. I will be using this daily. Still 5* is possible with some minor corrections to some simple errors.

I really enjoy the app, but the spherical zones where we have to put our hands into are just too small, making it too hard: I forget to check my footing and control my breathing because I can only focus on my hand positions

Devs: if you are reading this, can you please make the size/diameter of these hand position zones in the app's settings? Many reviewers have complained about this issue, and we haven't seen any actions to address this issue. Thanks!

reviews.review_title: Far too sensitive or just bad design
reviews.review_description:
I was looking forward to doing Tai Chi with my new Meta Quest 3.  As hard as I tried, I could not make it through the setup.  If the failed attempts gave me a description on how I failed, that may have helped. 

This app was a HUGE disappointment for me.

Update: The foot movements and breathing are not missing...the foot placement is often hard to see but there is a ghost image of an instructor that simulates the correct movement...I was to focused on the hand movements to notice this at first. The one recommendation I would suggest is to have some type of incentive to join and complete sessions daily with a tracker of stats...something that could be reviewed overtime. 

After reading the reviews for this app I was hesitant to purchase...but decided to give it a try. I was surprised the setup was not as bad as I read in the reviews. I got it setup by closely following the instructions and started to use it right way. I am not at all familiar with Tai Chi but get the idea by following arm movements but what I believe is missing is the foot movements and breathing properly. I would recommend a beginners guide to basic Tai Chi. The atmosphere and sounds are amazing! So much room for improvement...but if you are hesitant about this app I would suggest giving it a try...it is now on my daily routine!!!
Should have believed the reviews. Could never get started. Kept telling me I was off the line; which I absolutely was not. Need to delete aged want a refund. TRASH.
To the producers of this app, please fix it is by far too sensitive. You should have different levels of sensitivity; not to drive away potential customers! ￼

Yes, you do not have your headset charged up. Don’t even attempt download this thing or even it will do nothing for you at that point you will not be able to see it. All I was gonna do is give you a bunch of stuff on the screen where you’re saying, go into the game, but you can’t.

reviews.review_title: I hate this app so much
reviews.review_description: Tai Chi is supposed to be relaxing but every single time I try to use this app I get increasingly frustrated by the constant prompts telling me I’m doing it wrong. I know it’s not me. The tracking on this is so messed up. I will be following the movements exactly and it will say it’s wrong. And the hand tracking is almost non existent, it rarely even processes my movement’s. I have had this app for about 5 years now and I’ll randomly decided to give it another chance from time to time hoping it’s improved but it has never worked properly.
This app is so hard to set up. It keeps telling me I'm off the lio e but I'm not. It's impossible for me to follow the li e I guess. GRRRR

I’ll keep trying to get the setup to this app to work, as it’s exactly what was looking for. Tai chi moves you can follow along with either in VR or MR, with hand tracking.

But it refuses to calibrate correctly, seeming to thing my arms are much longer than they are, and that I’m shorter. I’m not a chimpanzee, honest!

I’m going to try clearing boundary history on the headset, and maybe a different practice space.

We shall see.

Have only had the app for a couple of days and at the moment it is increasing stress levels instead of calmness. The hand tracking while in the menu system is nearly unusable, the line coming out of the hands seems to have a minds of their own. Using the controllers in the sessions feels uncomfortable but when using hand tracking here looks and feels more natural.
The problem is that it regularly stops and tells me to recalibrate the arm lengths. I hope that later updates will improve things.
This app does not calibrate correctly. I either have to be on my knees the whole time or my arms need to be orangutan arms when the circles are in front of me. Can anyone help with the calibration?
works as it should and i enjoy using it the price is right it has great graphics

Update: although I think similarly about that word, I feel differently. It was just an intense time in the world… It is also intense now, but not so triggered. I think it’s a great app!

Hi! Just finished my first guided meditation. I like it a lot!! However, I’m using this app to help me with various forms of healing. I’m a survivor of gun violence and wish the word “Trigger” wasn’t used in a healing meditation app. Controller button or button, leaver, finger pad, index button, squeeze finger control, power activator, activator, actualizer all less “triggering” words that may be considered. Mindful practice and healing arts language can help VR industry and world be a better place. Thanks for thinking more on this. I personally teach drawing in VR. When I do the right controller is the “Hand of All-Power” and the left supporting controller is the “Hand of Infinite Options” .. perhaps you can see how much more empowering this is than squeeze the trigger..  Thank you!
Really like this

reviews.review_title: Not what I had hoped for....
reviews.review_description:
Years (and I mean YEARS) ago I did a Tai Chi class and learned a complete section - I think there were 3 in total. I learned the first one and used to do this 5 -10 minute routine at home and I loved it, then children etc came along and now they are grown up I wanted to learn again and thought this would be a great way of learning in my own time. I am completely distracted by the black and white balls, I can't seem to turn them off completely. What I remember is not being taught either, they are nothing like the tai chi I used to do, there were beautiful wrist movements etc, none of this is portrayed that I can see. I would like to learn what I remembered by following a teacher without the strict hand movements if I don't want them.  I come out more stressed than when I went in! I am sad because the surroundings are gorgeous, I just wish I could just follow a teacher and learn a routine that I can do with or without the VR.
UPDATE: 3 YEARS LATER...Tried again - I haven't played this since my first review (3 years ago), I thought I would see if there were any updates...a couple but far too few options, my opinion hasn't changed, I want to be able to look at the teacher and follow him without the distraction of the balls - PLEASE allow people to follow the teacher, I can see his hand movements and foot movements, why have him if you have to concentrate so hard on the bloomin' balls? PLEASE improve so people can just watch the teacher. It would be great if that could be done, I suspect it would be 5 stars from me if it could be as if I were in a real class. Thanks

reviews.review_title: Gets worse the more you play it
reviews.review_description: At first i liked it, but the glitches inctreased greatly the more i played. Thankfully my internal balance is good, othereise the glitches would have chaser me much injury. Refund me please.

May 28, 2025: 30 fantastical minutes of this Guided Tai Chi and I still want more. I find the 5-minute session so perfect as it allows me to get enough time to experience one at a time and vary between as many different sessions as I want. This possibility makes the experience even more enjoyable, uplifting, and engaging. I love it so much that, if not for the headset limitations in battery autonomy, I would keep practicing until my legs could no longer hold me upright🥰❤️. Kudos to the people behind this amazing application. Keep up the great work!

Left my controllers (somewhere) at my mom's house when I visited her out of state this past weekend. Bought this app last night, as I assumed a Tai Chi app would make use of hand tracking rather than controllers. Was surprised during the first step of setup (measuring my wingspan) when I had to use the controllers to squeeze the triggers, and the equivalent motions with hand tracking would not suffice.

This app is great, environments are fantastic. Hand tracking needs a bit of an adjustment but works pretty well while inside the flows. 
What I would wish is for longer programs, for now the sessions are 3-5minutes with the choice to take ”round the world tour” that can be 15, 30 or 60 minutes but which all consists of 5 minute flows in different worlds. This means there is no continuous flow to follow and you start over every 5 minutes.
For $6 on sale you really can't go wrong unless doing tai chi doesn't appeal to you.  Great thing to do on a break.

I like this app so far. I can think of one improvement that would make a big difference to me and that is to change the ball for hands. I can easily keep track of the hand and feet directions but also trying to look over to see which way around your palms should be is a little more difficult and seems to take a meat from the breathing and flow. 

Oh also why are they just 5 mins. I want to stay in one flow for at least half an hour. 5 mins is way too short to even get in the zone.

I really enjoy using this app. The different scenic locations to choose from are beautiful and really help to take you away from everyday life. I've never done tai chi before, but so far, I'm enjoying getting into it and practising. I can see how beneficial tai chi can potentially be as it requires balance and concentration. I think it will increase my overall body strength and balance as I use the app more. 

The tutorial was easy to follow, and once you've completed that, you're set to get going with the practice. 

I would like to suggest an alternative option to the developers however, that being an option to practice just following the instructors without the orbs. I find the orbs difficult to keep up with, and I find I'm concentrating on them more than what I'm doing with my hands, feet, and body. But I will probably get better at keeping up with the orbs with practice. But the option to practice without them would be great.

I would also really enjoy a longer practice session, but just with the instructors, and it would be great to have the narrator name the moves we're doing, only because I know nothing about tai chi and it would be great to understand what I'm doing. 

Overall, though, I love this app and would recommend it to anyone who wants a gentle exercise that will build body strength and balance, and that will help you relax and take you away from everyday life.

reviews.review_title: Great To Calm Down After Intense Gaming
reviews.review_description: I bought this on sale even after reading some negative reviews- I can’t say it’s jaw droppinging incredible- It’s nice, fun, and with some patience, enjoyable. All of us need to focus on the breath and get in a flow state, this does it for me! The only thing is no explanation of why those moves - but that’s my critique of a lot of games- you need YouTube to figure it out -but the actual game play is soothing -

I have had this for over a year now and enjoy the guided practice. I am  not exactly graceful or co-ordinated, but on this platform it doesn’t matter. I can regain my composure and continue on with my practice without disturbing anyone.  I believe I have actually have improved my balance and breathing techniques.

I’m glad I ignored the reviews and bought this app especially while it’s on sale! It’s obvious the developers put a lot of effort into this one. There’s a learning curve which only makes sense but so worth it. I’m currently using this app to stretch in the morning and to cool down after workouts. It’d be nice before bed too.  I like how the music is more clear when you’re getting it right, I like the environments and the endless amount of sessions to choose from. This company also makes a meditation app which has more options than any other meditation app I’ve seen, that is also part of the holiday sale at a ridiculously low price called “Guided Meditation”. Both of these apps are already at a fair price…so if you’re into mindfulness or just feeling good, it’d be crazy to pass them by.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the kind review, Nahli.Marie! I really made our day. ✨

We really do put a ton of love and care into our apps. While we respect that they aren't for everyone, we're hopeful we can make life brighter for some. It's a genuine honor to be part of your daily routine! 💕

reviews.review_description: This is so grounding and a wonderful start to the day, gentle movements, loosens the joints, unwinds the mind, as another review said, great either end of the day, or to de-stress, brilliant value for money, always wanted to do Tai chi, and I'm going to get my zen on!

I have been using this for quite a while and absolutely loved it, until a recent update made it unusable. Hand tracking will not work, even in a well-lit area, and likewise the hand devices don't track correctly. I have tried rebooting my Meta Quest 2 and it made no difference at all.
Until that update, I would have rated this program as five stars.

That said, I have no clue if it's helping me in any way. I continue to do it though. I work out in Les Mills Body Combat, then Supernatural, then this, then Yoga VR every day. I'm definitely in less pain post workout since doing this and Yoga. I don't know if it's helpful in other ways. Not sure I'm learning anything new. I really wish the developers would improve on it by giving some instruction. However it seems they created it and dropped it into the store, no further development appears to be happening. I also see people mentioning a tutorial. I never found a tutorial in this. Plus it seems to think I'm 6'5"...nope, I'm 5'6". Visiting their website is so helpful (NOT)...go ahead try it. LOL. Obviously there will be no further development.

Update 12/29/2024: I was wrong, they did something! I opened the app and it asked me to calibrate my arm length! I don't have to be 7' tall anymore! Yay! I bumped it up a star.

I've bought this app on sale a couple of days ago. This app deserves, in my opinion, more praise than what it has gotten. The sceneries are beautiful and calming, and the music is relaxing. And I find the handtracking works OK - even if it's a littlebit unusual having "the sensors"/beemes coming out of my wrists. What I wish the developers would do different, is to let the body of the instructor in the front, be see-through, so I can see how he holds his hands, without turning to watch the instructor on the side. (now I can see his hands through his head, but not through his body). I'm also wondering if there is a quick way to exit/go back to the menu, once I've started a Flow? Thank you for an beautiful app.

The tracking is just very bad.  Stopped even trying to lay it a couple years ago and then recently saw the hand tracking addition so tried it again and..... still bad.  Some seem to like it but for me, its just an awful app.
I don't understand the negative reviews, this experience is unique

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the kind thoughts, Myo! ✨

reviews.review_description: As a tai chi teacher, I think calling this "Guided Tai Chi" is a reach. The movements are primitive and of little variety. The model footwork is clumsy. The pacing is fine, so once one can follow the orbs without thinking the app may serve as a moving meditation, but tai chi is so much more. The real movements of any style are vastly more complex, and mastering them precisely takes us to the rewards of tai chi. So I gave one star for the moving meditation, but there is nothing else to commend.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your thoughts!

When we worked with Tai Chi experts, one thing we had to keep in mind was that the device can't track hands behind the player's view or that the average player has 5ft of space to enjoy a session. That's why we list this as inspired by Tai Chi, and not a 1-to-1 recreation.

The value in Guided Tai Chi is that it builds off of the traditional Tai Chi short form, while working within limits that make it viable and engaging for all players – not just those looking for mastery.

Ultimately, we feel that exposing more people to the concept of movement meditations is a net benefit, even if we have to break rules to do so. ✨

reviews.review_title: Not what I was looking for
reviews.review_description: I was imagining a group tai chi setting where I can see other people make the movements while I try to mimic them. This was more of a make your hands follow paths kind of thing. I’m not even sure what it had to do with tai chi other than some vaguely relaxing setting.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback, Kabal!

It sounds like you're looking for Flow Mode, which has teachers in front and to the sides performing the moves which you can mirror. To ensure they're there, you may want to look into Options and make show 'Show Teachers' is checked. You can also optionally set them to face towards or away from you.

On the movements, these are all inspired by the Tai Chi 24 form. They're modified though to work best with the Quest's tracking area and the average player's space.

Yesterday we launched Mixed Reality mode, alongside hundreds of other refinements. We hope you have a chance to take peek! ✨

reviews.review_description: The idea is great.  The developer should focus on simplicity.  The basic yang movements are ideal for simple illustration.  People that would use this app and keep up are mostly adults.  Keep that in mind.  Suggest, scrap out all the unnecessary commands and options.  Keep it on yang method.  Provide option to follow instruction’s back or face-to-face.  Keep everything else simple.

For 9.99 it is a good kindergarten intro. It is not tai chi which is sad because it could be the best tai chi learning program ever. Please go to the great teachers who have really good video programs and instruction and merge it with oculus and you will sell millions at 99.99.tai chi needs virtual reality because video is too two dimensional and oculus needs a great program that is tai chi not a baby game. Tai chi is practiced by millions world wide and is growing in popularity in the west you have a potential gold mine if you spend just a little time and money. I would be glad to help you if you wish.  Just look on YouTube and see many great tc videos couple that with your environment  music voice over holograms and you will be rich beyond imagination.

Just bought the game today and hand tracking is not working no matter what I do, my hands are just not showing. And there is no way to get support otherthan writing a review and wait for a response, I can't get the customer support email address.

Unable to play as the game does not correctly put me in the centre of the guardian space, only right at the front, so if I reach forward at all, the barrier comes up. There are no options to fix this.

reviews.review_title: Can't see hands like it says you can
reviews.review_description:
I bought this to try and relieve stress, but in the hand tutorial I did as was told but cant see what the tutorial says I should be able to.

If this doesn't resolve soon I will be having to get refund

If you are old, like me, you have probably played one of those old-time games where you have to loop a ring on a stick through a wire track. If you touched the wire with the ring, a buzzer would go off and you would have to start over.

That's how this experience feels. There is nothing relaxing about it. Keep an eye on where the game wants you to put your hands, moving them sloooowly, and figure out how to keep track on what your posture is supposed to be by looking somewhere else as well. It's too much, too slow and not remotely like actual Tai Chi (for me, anyway).

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your useful feedback!❤️

You might enjoy Flow Mode, where there are no points and posture is learned by watching your teachers. We'll also be launching a new mode called Pure, which is only you and the teacher without any orbs or paths for those looking for a more traditional experience.

We hope you have a chance to try this and it is more your cup of tea!

reviews.review_description:
I want to give a good review. The music and backgrounds are amazing. But that is where it stops.
Apart from the tutorial there are 3 game options.
Normal, follow the orbs and know there are a few masters nearby doing the exercises with you. Though you can't see what they are doing and you keep losing track of the orbs you need to follow.
Arcade. Identical to the first apart from shorter and you get points.
Around the world, full hour. Haven't tried that yet.

Here is the big problem. I have no bloody clue what movements i need to do. The voice with pointless sayings gets on my nerves and i keep losing track on the balls cause i am not a chameleon.

This game could be great. Just add another game mode called training, remove the balls and turn the master infront of you around, then mirror him.
Let us see what he is doing and learn the hand and foot work. 
Then we can always move on to touching the balls while not seeing anything else.

And hearing the voice say good work when you have one hand on a ball while waving around to gind the real life location of the other is not helpful. It is grating and teeth grinding. 

I have never spend so much time cursing a non existing voice and while it is officially a calming game, i leave the game more stressed and pissed then i go into the game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback!❤️

When we started this project we learned something unsettling: it takes the average Tai Chi student a half dozen years to become well versed. For the average teacher, it can take decades, with daily practice.

Our mission was to demystify what we could. How could we allow someone to jump into Tai Chi right now? 

Your idea to remove the lines in FLOW MODE is interesting! We will be adding in an option soon to adjust the balls to hands and turn off the teacher audio, so adding lines wouldn't be a problem. Thank you!

This month we'll be launching a massive update which doubles the features, modes, and environments. We hope you give Guided Tai Chi another try once this goes live!😊

reviews.review_description: Purchased this game but I don't see that it teaches Tai Chi at all.  You get transported to a pretty place and then you're supposed to trace the yin and yang lines with your arms.  So I move around a lot, stepping back and forth and trying to keep up.  It actually hurts my back from leaning forward.  There's a guy next to me, showing me how to do it but there's no way to watch him and follow the paths.   Not much to it.  Wish I could get my money back!

The game completely disregards the guardian.  I will be standing in the middle of the guardian (set to >6.5'x6.5', click to start, and it pops the feet at the very edge of the guardian right next to a countertop and potted plant.  Just touching the dots initially gets at least 1 hand giving a guardian warning, then it immediately pushes both hands outside the guardian.  EVERY TIME.  I've repeatedly felt the leaves of the plant on the counter, which is well beyond the guardian.

Under NO circumstances should a GUIDED game be capable of guiding your body parts beyond that guardian.  That's just dangerous.

developer_response.body:
Guided Tai Chi allows you to begin your session where ever you choose within your play space. If you choose a location near the edge of the guardian, the guardian will display. If you’re seeing the guardian, take a step back from the center to allow your forward movements to not trigger this. Oculus apps have no control over the guardian display, or your physical placement within the guardian space.

Guided Tai Chi also supports standing mode, which reduces the forward motion capture movements for those with smaller play spaces. We’d recommend testing both of these built-in options to find which works best for you.

reviews.review_description: I really love the idea of having this in VR but hand and foot instructions should be coordinated. Very difficult to follow hands and feet at the same time. Hoping this issue will be updated for more realistic tai chi experience and work-out.

Just bought my Oculus 2 headset. Started a tai chi and meditation practice in the mornings. I enjoy this tai chi program. I’m not sure how accurate it is to regular tai chi but it seems to be helping me focus and relax. I cannot figure out how to use this app without the controllers. I watched the video multiple times and do what I’m told but it doesn’t work.
I wouldn’t get this

reviews.review_title: Please sort out the hand tracking
reviews.review_description:
Love this app. Beautiful locations and calming music. But it has three negatives which is why I've only given it three stars. 
1. The hand tracking just doesn't work so have to use the controllers which feels heavy and wrong for Thai Chi. 
2. The arm length is wrong, it's either too long and I can't reach or I shorten it and everything becomes child sized.
3. Many of the moves go outside of the guardian boundary which is very distracting.
Fix these and it'll be a great app.
Also personally I'd prefer to have the guide face me and mirror the moves so it's easier to learn. Maybe with an option to have a solid guide rather than ghost... a few different options for all levels.
May be a few beginners levels teaching the various Thai chi moves would be good too

reviews.review_title: Who knew I would love this so much
reviews.review_description: I've been wanting to try this for years, but at 58 and 260lbs, I'm not the most graceful man.  I also hate classes.   This makes it easy, and a great way to end a day.  Love this app!

No matter how many times you set the teacher in settings to face you he is always facing away, I have found putting a YouTube video on works a lot better as instructions. 

Also there is a layering problem with the menus that causes the cursor to be BEHIND  the menu rather the on it, making it so you can't see what you are pointing at.

reviews.review_title: Can we have a couple of dots please?
reviews.review_description:
I've only just started so can't leave a proper review but so far I like what I see. This is more of a request. Can you please add a way of showing hand orientation on the guide balls. It breaks the focus when I keep having to look left and right to see what position the hands should be in. Maybe a black dot on the white ball and a white dot on the black one that shows the direction that the palm should be facing. 

Thank you 🙂

Once I've put in a good few hours I'll come back and leave a proper review. 

Namaste x
I bought this because of the option for hand recognition without the controllers, but can’t seem to get it to work.

I've noticed the app has many negative reviews regarding hand tracking not working! This is unfortunate as it seems to be lack of knowledge on the user's end but is no fault of their own. In order to use hand tracking, you must first enable it on your oculus device in settings -> hand tracking. Enable hand tracking as well as automatic transition. Then, open the app again and you should be good to go! The developers should definitely add a disclaimer that you must enable this feature on your device before the "magic" can begin.

reviews.review_title: I want to see my real hands
reviews.review_description: Why not to have an option which turns the background skybox to the headset’s cameras? I would love to see my hands and body movements. Please add this feature. I love the App. Highly recommended

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your feedback!

Using the Quest's cameras isn't currently allowed within an app, just for the home environment.

We will be adding hand tracking support within the next week or so. We're testing this now, alongside 11 new environments, new modes, and dozens of improvements.

Thank you again for your feedback and wishing you happy adventures ahead in Guided Tai Chi!

reviews.review_description: Looking forward to enjoying this well thought out and beautiful app. However when the first session started, the spheres were at my knee level! Stayed at that low level throughout the 5 minute session. I did the arm length calibration already. Have hand tracking enabled, which seemed to wok very well. Is this easily resolved? Apart from this glitch, the exercise felt or will feel appropriate for wrapping up the workout I’d done prior. Or late in the evening before sleep. I imagine others might enjoy it to start their day as well.

reviews.review_title: You're better off with YouTube VR
reviews.review_description:
First I'll go over what's wrong with the app, mainly the orbs. The ability to turn off orbs and just follow the teacher that'd be great. I see a setting to not show orbs and path on line and I thought that would turn it off but that did nothing, I don't know what that setting means. Would be nice if there was descriptions on the setting options. The orbs are annoying. You go too far away the game pauses and asks if you need to recalibrate plus the music fades in and out as you get away. I had to turn the music off which sucks because I like the music. Don't fix it and I'm requesting a refund because I literally can't play this game, the constant asking if I need to recalibrate plus the trying to follow the orbs is annoying, disruptive and it just doesn't feel relaxing at all.

Now what's wrong with app even if it functioned properly. It's not Tai chi. These are not Tai chi moves. Not that it isn't relaxing to do whatever it is you are doing. If you want real Tai chi in VR use the YouTube app and watch a real Tai chi master and follow them. Not only is that better than this but VR for workout videos is just absolutely amazing. Try it.

In tai chi foot placement as well as breath is very important just like yoga unfortunately this app has no instructions on those and expects you to follow along with the ghost people beside you. It also goes out of bounds and has no idea of arm length. You end up spending more time focusing on keeping your hands in the circle then actually breathing or relaxing.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback!

Good news: Guided VR does provide foot guidance, with detailed foot settings within options. This is displayed both on the ground and at eye level when activated.

Breath guidance is shared in Flow mode, alongside movement, grounding, flow, and other tips. Arcade mode does not feature a guide, so no breath feedback is shared.

Arm length is calculated based on height, but it can also be manually configured within options to work best for you.

To avoid going out of bounds it is best to begin a session in the center or back center of your space, as movements forward can be quite large.

If looking to relax further and you're familiar with yoga or Tai Chi, you may enjoy "explore mode" which is hidden behind your starting view in the main menu. This allows you to simply be in a scene without guidance, at your own pace.

We hope these tips help you enjoy your experience even further!❤️
